steps.title: Find what historians say about pachuca style
steps.content: The passage states historians see pachuca style “as a form of rebellion against the era’s rigid social expectations for women” and that “it showed a desire for self-expression and freedom.” That’s the historian view we need to match.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘similar to other fashion trends that different groups of women adopted’: The passage never compares pachuca style to other groups’ fashion trends. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘celebrated because it enabled women to show support for the United States during WWII’: The passage says people criticized pachuca style; there’s no mention of patriotic support. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘continues to influence fashion in the United States to the present day’: No modern influence is mentioned anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘a way for some Mexican American women to express themselves and resist strict social expectations’: Directly matches “self-expression and freedom” and “rebellion against rigid social expectations.” ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
